sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stephens Upgrades Boot Barn Holdings, Inc. (BOOT) to Overweight from Equal Weight and Raises its Price Target to $237
feed.summary: Stephens upgraded Boot Barn Holdings, Inc. (BOOT) to Overweight from Equal Weight, raising its price target to $237. The upgrade reflects the firm's confidence in Boot Barn's earnings growth model and management, supported by strong third-quarter results and positive sales outlooks from analysts like BofA's Christopher Nardone. The company reported revenue of $705.643 million and guided fiscal 2026 revenue to $2.24 billion to $2.25 billion.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (KVUE) Kenvue Inc. Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.27 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.22
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. (KVUE) announced its Q4 adjusted EPS was $0.27 per share, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $0.22. Additionally, the company reported Q4 revenue of $3.78 billion, which also exceeded the FactSet estimate of $3.68 billion. This positive financial performance indicates stronger-than-expected results for the consumer healthcare products leader.

feed.title: Fortive at Citi’s Conference: Strategic Growth Insights
feed.summary: Fortive Corporation presented at Citi's Global Industrial Tech & Mobility Conference 2026, where CEO Olumide Soroye and CFO Mark Åkerström discussed the company's commitment to achieving 3%-4% core organic growth and expanding adjusted EBITDA margins in 2026 and 2027. The company emphasized its strategic initiatives, including AI integration across operations and products, disciplined capital allocation prioritizing strong free cash flow, and maintaining investor trust. Fortive highlighted strong performance in its IOS segment driven by product innovation and recurring customer value, while also addressing market dynamics in North America, EMEA, and APAC, and strategic approaches to M&A and investment in worker safety businesses.

feed.title: (CSR) Centerspace Expects Full Year 2026 Core FFO Range $4.81 - $5.05, vs. FactSet Est of $4.99
feed.summary: Centerspace (CSR) has announced its expectation for a Full Year 2026 Core Funds From Operations (FFO) to be in the range of $4.81 to $5.05. This forecast aligns closely with the FactSet estimate of $4.99. The announcement reflects the company's financial outlook for the upcoming year in the residential REIT sector.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems's (NASDAQ:CDNS) Q4 CY2025: Beats On Revenue
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS) reported better-than-expected revenue of $1.44 billion in Q4 CY2025, a 6.2% increase year-on-year, and exceeded analyst estimates for adjusted EPS at $1.99. The company also provided optimistic EPS guidance for the upcoming financial year. Despite a slight deceleration in projected revenue growth, Cadence Design Systems demonstrated strong customer acquisition efficiency and sound financial health.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics, SGH Raise BlueScope Steel Takeover Bid to $11 Billion
feed.summary: U.S. steelmaker Steel Dynamics and Australian conglomerate SGH have increased their takeover bid for BlueScope Steel to approximately $11 billion. The revised offer values BlueScope shareholders at A$32.35, or US$22.93, per share in cash, a significant increase from the previous December offer of A$30 per share, which was valued at roughly US$8.8 billion.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Purchases Shares of VICI Properties Inc. (NYSE:VICI)
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. (D-California) recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of VICI Properties Inc. (NYSE:VICI) stock on January 9th, as disclosed in a February 13th filing. VICI Properties, a real estate investment trust specializing in experiential real estate, has a market cap of approximately $31.9 billion and recently paid a quarterly dividend of $0.45 per share, yielding about 6.0%. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for the stock with an average price target of $33.85.

feed.title: Plano offers $20M in incentives for AT&T’s new multibillion-dollar HQ
feed.summary: Plano is offering AT&T a $20 million incentive package and a 65% property tax rebate over 25 years to establish its new global headquarters in the city. The telecom giant must invest a minimum of $1.4 billion in construction, build 2 million square feet of space, employ 10,000 workers, and occupy the site for 25 years. This move is expected to have significant economic implications for downtown Dallas, AT&T's previous home.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Buys Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE:PRU) Shares
feed.summary: Representative Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. recently purchased between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE:PRU) stock on January 9th. This disclosure was made on February 13th, with the transaction occurring in his "150 MAIN STREET TRUST > BANK OF AMERICA" account. Prudential Financial also recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.40 and reported its latest quarterly earnings, which missed analyst EPS estimates but exceeded revenue expectations.

feed.title: Kenvue: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter results, with profits of $330 million and adjusted earnings of 27 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The consumer health company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $3.78 billion for the quarter. For the full year, Kenvue reported a profit of $1.47 billion and revenue of $15.12 billion.

feed.title: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Agilon Health, Inc.
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. reminds Agilon Health, Inc. investors with significant losses of the upcoming March 2nd lead plaintiff deadline for a class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Agilon made false and misleading statements regarding its 2025 guidance and the positive financial impact of its "strategic actions." Investors who purchased Agilon securities between February 26, 2025, and August 4, 2025, are urged to contact the firm to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: Is Oracle a Once-in-a-Decade Buying Opportunity Right Now?
feed.summary: Oracle's stock price has fallen since its major deal with OpenAI, despite a huge backlog in its cloud infrastructure. The company is investing heavily in cloud computing for AI but faces significant risks, including burning cash for infrastructure build-out and a heavy reliance on its single OpenAI contract, which doesn't start until 2027. While its stock appears cheap relative to competitors, analysts suggest its higher uncertainty warrants a lower earnings multiple.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Welcomes HOPE Inside for Financial Coaching
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has partnered with the national nonprofit Operation HOPE to open a HOPE Inside center in Midtown Anchorage. This initiative aims to provide free financial education workshops and one-on-one coaching to community members, empowering them to achieve financial goals. This new center is part of Wells Fargo's broader commitment to launching fifty such centers in low-to-middle-income communities by the end of the year, enhancing financial inclusion and access to essential financial tools.

feed.title: SGH and Steel Dynamics submit A$15B final offer for BlueScope Steel
feed.summary: SGH and Steel Dynamics have submitted a final, non-binding offer of A$15 billion to acquire BlueScope Steel. This move indicates a significant consolidation effort in the steel industry, with major players vying for control and market share. The offer's terms and Bluescope's response will determine the future landscape of the steel market.

feed.title: Bill Ackman Goes Magnificent Seven Shopping In Q4: Adds META, Increases AMZN, Decreases GOOGL
feed.summary: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital made significant changes to its portfolio in Q4, notably adding a new stake in Meta Platforms and increasing its position in Amazon.com. The fund also decreased its exposure to Alphabet Inc. shares for the second consecutive quarter. These adjustments reflect Ackman's continued focus on "Magnificent Seven" stocks, with Meta and Amazon becoming top holdings due to perceived long-term potential in AI and digital advertising.

feed.title: Realty Income (O) Maintains Monthly Dividend with 4.87% Yield
feed.summary: Realty Income (O) has declared a consistent monthly dividend of $0.27 per share, yielding 4.87%, with strong financial metrics including consistent revenue growth and a solid operating margin. Despite robust institutional ownership, the company's valuation metrics suggest it may be trading near historical highs, and its Altman Z-Score indicates potential financial challenges. Investors are advised to balance the company's reliable income generation with vigilance regarding valuation and financial health indicators.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment, Inc. reported a Q4 loss of $250 million, or $1.23 per share, which was worse than Wall Street expectations of a 33-cent loss per share. Despite the loss, the company's revenue of $2.92 billion exceeded analyst forecasts. For the full year, Caesars Entertainment reported a loss of $502 million on revenue of $11.49 billion.

feed.title: Ball Corporation to Present at Global Agriculture and Materials Conference
feed.summary: Ball Corporation’s CEO Ron Lewis and CFO Dan Rabbitt are scheduled to present at the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference on February 26, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The company, a global leader in sustainable aluminum packaging solutions, reported net sales of $13.16 billion in 2025 and operates over 70 manufacturing plants worldwide. A live webcast will be available for investors and stakeholders to attend the presentation.

feed.title: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith Encourages PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Shareholders To Inquire About Securities Fraud Class Action
authors: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Howard G. Smith are encouraging shareholders of PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) to inquire about a securities fraud class action lawsuit filed against the company. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal made materially false and misleading statements about its business operations and execution on initiatives between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. This follows a significant drop in PayPal's stock price after a surprise leadership change and an admission of poor execution in branded checkout.

feed.title: Demystifying eBay: Insights From 4 Analyst Reviews
feed.summary: Over the last three months, four analysts have provided diverse ratings for eBay (NASDAQ: EBAY), with an average 12-month price target of $104.5, reflecting a 2.45% increase from the previous target. The article highlights eBay's financial performance, including an impressive revenue growth rate of 9.47% as of September 2025, high net margin, and strong ROE and ROA. Analyst ratings and target adjustments offer insights into the company's market standing and financial health, emphasizing the importance of understanding these evaluations alongside financial indicators.

feed.title: ATS to Participate in the Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference
feed.summary: ATS Corporation (TSX: ATS) (NYSE: ATS) announced that its CEO, Doug Wright, and Interim CFO, Anne Cybulski, will participate in the Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 3, 2026, in Orlando, FL. They are scheduled to host a fireside chat at 2:15 p.m. (ET), with a webcast available on the Investor Relations site. Management will also be available for institutional investor meetings at the conference.

feed.title: Caesar's Stock Climbs After Mixed Q4 Earnings Report, Record Digital EBITDA
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ: CZR) reported mixed fourth-quarter earnings, with a loss of 33 cents per share against an estimated loss of 17 cents, but revenue beat expectations at $2.92 billion. The company achieved a record $85 million in adjusted EBITDA for Caesars Digital. Following the report, Caesar's stock rose 3.75% in extended trading.

feed.title: Public Storage Will Relocate from Longtime SoCal Headquarters to Texas
feed.summary: Self-storage REIT Public Storage announced its corporate headquarters will move from Glendale, California, where it has been since 1972, to Frisco, Texas. The company stated this relocation is part of its PS4.0 strategic vision, aimed at leveraging North Texas's talent pool and supporting its growth, though it will retain a presence in Glendale. CFO and CIO Tom Boyle, soon to be CEO, highlighted the company's strong performance, digital advancements, and scalable growth within a fragmented market.

feed.title: Cyclerion To Advance CYC-126 Into Phase 2 Study For Treatment-resistant Depression
feed.summary: Cyclerion Therapeutics (CYCN) has received positive FDA feedback to advance its investigational therapy, CYC-126, into a Phase 2 study for treatment-resistant depression (TRD). The company plans to initiate the study in the second half of 2026, leveraging a design that incorporates real-time EEG feedback and algorithm-guided dosing. An advisory board has been formed to provide strategic guidance for the development of CYC-126.

feed.title: News | Armada Hoffler moves to shed multifamily portfolio, rebrand as AH Realty Trust
feed.summary: Armada Hoffler Properties is undergoing a major strategic shift, rebranding as AH Realty Trust and divesting its multifamily portfolio and construction arm. This repositioning aims to focus on its retail and office core, reduce its $1.5 billion debt, and generate more predictable cash flows, with significant asset sales anticipated in 2026. The company intends to use sale proceeds for debt reduction and has budgeted $50 million for retail acquisitions in its target markets.

feed.title: Bridgeport man accused of stealing 26 tons of aluminum
feed.summary: A Bridgeport man, Ramon Penali-Villar, has been accused of stealing 26 tons of aluminum from Olympic Steel Company. He turned himself in after allegedly committing the theft in November with Eddy Ramirez, who was arrested in January. Authorities successfully recovered all of the stolen aluminum.

feed.title: SGH, Steel Dynamics bid $10.6 billion for BlueSCope Steel in 'best and final' offer
feed.summary: SGH, Steel Dynamics have made a "best and final" offer of $10.6 billion to acquire BlueScope Steel. This move indicates a significant potential merger in the steel industry. The article is a brief news update from Reuters.

feed.title: EQT Corp beats quarterly profit estimates on higher natgas prices
feed.summary: EQT Corp (EQT.N) exceeded Wall Street's adjusted profit estimates for the fourth quarter, driven by increased natural gas prices and higher sales volumes. The company reported an adjusted profit of 90 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations of 74 cents, as average natural gas prices rose by 14.3% and total sales volumes increased year-over-year. This performance was attributed to rising demand from power-hungry data centers and a surge in liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace files for three-part notes offering of up to $1.2 bln
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) has announced a proposed three-part notes offering that could raise up to $1.2 billion. The company, a provider of advanced engineered solutions for the aerospace and transportation industries, made this announcement via an SEC filing. This offering follows recent analyst adjustments to price targets for Howmet Aerospace from firms like UBS, Goldman Sachs, and Jefferies, most of which maintain positive ratings.

feed.title: Avino Silver & Gold Mines (NYSE:ASM) Trading Down 12.2% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Avino Silver & Gold Mines (NYSE:ASM) saw its shares drop by 12.2% on Tuesday, with significantly lower trading volume than its daily average. Despite the price dip, the company maintains strong financials with good liquidity and negligible debt, though its P/E ratio is very high. Analyst ratings are mixed, resulting in a "Moderate Buy" consensus but individual targets vary widely.

feed.title: Warner Bros. Discovery: Acquisition Talks Reopened for Paramount as Vote on Netflix Offer Is Set
feed.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery has reopened acquisition talks with Paramount Skydance, even as a vote on Netflix's offer for a portion of WBD's business approaches. Paramount is reportedly willing to increase its offer to acquire all of Warner, currently at $30 per share, while Netflix has offered $27.75 per share for the streaming and studios businesses only. Morningstar maintains its fair value estimates for WBD, Netflix, and Paramount, expecting WBD to be acquired at over $30 per share, though neither Netflix nor Paramount has a clear majority chance of success.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE: McCann Wins Kroger's US Creative Business
feed.summary: McCann has been named the creative agency of record for Kroger U.S., taking over from DDB New York. This move keeps the Kroger account within the Omnicom umbrella, following Omnicom's acquisition of IPG, McCann's former parent company. McCann New York's CEO, Amber Guild, expressed pride in partnering with Kroger, an iconic brand, to develop meaningful work and drive business growth.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend on Common Stock, Payable on May 6, 2026
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. (NYSE: WST) has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share on its common stock. This dividend is payable on May 6, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 29, 2026. The announcement was made on February 16, 2026, by the company's Board of Directors.

feed.title: SGH, Steel Dynamics raise buyout offer for BlueScope to $10.6B
feed.summary: SGH Ltd and Steel Dynamics have increased their takeover bid for BlueScope Steel to A$15 billion ($10.62 billion), calling it their "best and final" offer. The new offer of A$32.35 per share in cash represents a 15.5% premium over their previous bid and excludes dividends. The companies stated this offer is final unless a superior competing proposal emerges.

feed.title: Kenvue (NYSE: KVUE) details 2025 results, $250M restructuring and Kimberly-Clark deal progress
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. announced its full-year 2025 financial results, reporting a decline in net sales and organic sales but an increase in diluted EPS. The company also detailed a new restructuring initiative expected to cut its global workforce by 3.5% and result in $250 million in pre-tax charges for fiscal year 2026. Additionally, Kenvue provided updates on its pending acquisition by Kimberly-Clark, noting shareholder approvals and U.S. antitrust clearance, with closing anticipated in the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Kenvue Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. announced its financial results for the fiscal fourth quarter and full year ended December 28, 2025, showing strong top- and bottom-line performance in the fourth quarter. The company attributes its success to disciplined execution against strategic priorities and a favorable year-ago comparison on sales, as stated by CEO Kirk Perry. Kenvue also confirmed its pending acquisition by Kimberly-Clark, which received overwhelming shareholder approval in January 2026 and is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

feed.title: GLTO: UBS Initiates Coverage with a 'Buy' Rating and $45 PT | GL
feed.summary: UBS has initiated coverage on Galecto (GLTO) with a 'Buy' rating and a price target of $45.00. This follows recent positive analyst coverage from Leerink Partners and Guggenheim. Galecto is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing therapeutics for cancer and liver diseases, including treatments for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) and non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH).

feed.title: Kenvue Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 28, 2025, showing improved performance with Q4 net sales up 3.2% and organic sales growing by 1.2%. The company reported diluted EPS of $0.17 for Q4 and $0.76 for the full year 2025. Kenvue also provided an update on its pending acquisition by Kimberly-Clark, which is expected to close in the second half of 2026, and confirmed it will not provide forward-looking guidance due to this transaction.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean’s Perfect Day Mexico Project Faces Protest and Court-Ordered Halt
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean’s Perfect Day Mexico project is facing protests from local residents demanding improved infrastructure and services, and has been partially halted by a court injunction. The injunction, secured by an NGO, challenges the legality of a land use change for the project without public consultation, despite the government's denial. Concerns also exist about the project's environmental impact on mangroves and the town's water supply, though Royal Caribbean states it will protect conservation areas.

feed.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. Announces March 2026 Dividend Rate per Common Share
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. (NYSE: ARR and ARR-PRC) has announced a cash dividend of $0.24 per common share for March 2026. The dividend will be paid on March 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. As a REIT, ARMOUR is required to distribute most of its taxable income, and dividends exceeding current earnings and profits are generally not taxable to common stockholders.

feed.title: Georgia Power says filings with Georgia Public Service Commission propose decrease in rates from June
feed.summary: Georgia Power has submitted filings to the Georgia Public Service Commission proposing a decrease in electricity rates starting in June. This development was published on February 17, 2026, and is relevant to customers of the utility. The article also provides recent news and financial data related to Southern Company, Georgia Power's parent company, including dividend announcements and analyst ratings.

feed.title: Ball Corporation to Present at Bank of America's 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference
feed.summary: Ball Corporation (NYSE: BALL) announced it will present at the Bank of America 2026 Global Agriculture and Materials Conference on February 26, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time. CEO Ron Lewis and CFO Dan Rabbitt are scheduled to speak. A live webcast of the presentation will be available.

feed.title: US$11bn all-cash offer aims to split BlueScope between SGH and SDI
feed.summary: SGH Ltd (ASX:SGH) and Steel Dynamics, Inc. (NASDAQ/GS:STLD) have submitted a best-and-final all-cash offer of A$32.35 per share to acquire BlueScope Steel Ltd (ASX:BSL), valuing the company at A$15 billion (US$11 billion). If the acquisition is successful, SGH plans to retain BlueScope's Australia and Rest of World assets while on-selling its North American operations to Steel Dynamics. The offer represents significant premiums to BlueScope's trading benchmarks but is subject to due diligence, shareholder, and regulatory approvals.

feed.title: One airline is about to bring a big increase in first-class seats to SFO
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is planning to significantly increase the number of first-class seats available on its flights between Atlanta and San Francisco International Airport (SFO). This move is expected to benefit SFO summer travelers looking for premium experiences on this key business route. The article suggests this strategy is a response to regulatory circumstances.

feed.title: Republic Services (NYSE:RSG) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.14 EPS
feed.summary: Republic Services (NYSE:RSG) reported Q4 EPS of $1.76, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.14, although revenue of $4.14 billion missed the $4.21 billion consensus. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.625 per share. Shares traded down following the announcement, but analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $248.82.

feed.title: Twilio, Zscaler, Snowflake, UiPath, and GitLab Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Twilio, Zscaler, Snowflake, UiPath, and GitLab fell in the afternoon session due to investor fears that artificial intelligence could disrupt the software industry. This "AI Panic" led to a broad sell-off across the sector, driven by concerns over new 'agentic AI' tools potentially dismantling traditional SaaS business models. The market's reaction reflects growing anxiety about the impact of the AI boom on established software companies.

feed.title: Devon Energy reports in-line Q4 earnings, revenue beats estimates
feed.summary: Devon Energy Corp. reported Q4 2025 adjusted earnings per share of $0.82, matching analyst expectations, while revenue of $4.12 billion surpassed estimates. The oil and gas producer's stock slipped 0.7% following the announcement. The company provided Q1 2026 production guidance, reflecting a 1% reduction due to winter weather, and is preparing for its strategic merger with Coterra.

feed.title: Insuring the Future: Brown & Brown Names New Technology Chief
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. has appointed Dorothea (“Dori”) Henderson as its new Chief Information Technology Officer. Based in Daytona Beach, Florida, Henderson will lead the company's enterprise technology strategy, focusing on enhancing digital capabilities, improving the technology experience for teammates and customers, and advancing a unified data and AI strategy. President and CEO Powell Brown emphasized that Henderson's strategic perspective and operational discipline are crucial at this pivotal time for the company.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (WTTR) Select Water Solutions Posts Q4 Breakeven EPS, vs. FactSet Est of $0.03 EPS
feed.summary: Select Water Solutions (WTTR) reported breakeven earnings per share for the fourth quarter, falling short of the FactSet estimate of $0.03 EPS. The company's revenue for the quarter also reached $346.5 million, exceeding the FactSet estimate of $321.6 million.

feed.title: Investor Notice: Robbins LLP Informs Investors of the PayPal Holdings, Inc. Class Action
feed.summary: Robbins LLP has filed a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors who purchased PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) securities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal misled investors regarding its projected revenue growth and the achievability of its 2027 targets for Branded Checkout offerings. Following the announcement of disappointing Q4 and full fiscal year 2025 financial results and a CEO change, PayPal's stock price dropped significantly.
